explanation: This command simulates launching a browser (chrome.exe) from a document reader process (AcrobatReader.exe) with a command line containing 'http'. It aligns with the Sigma rule's detection criteria.
This command uses reordering and omission techniques to bypass detection. The '&' operator replaces '&&' for process chaining, and the URL is directly passed without specifying a tab creation flag.
This command uses substitution (short/long options) and insertion (quotes) to simulate a browser launch from a document reader process. It maintains the same behavior but uses different syntax.
This command omits optional flags (e.g., --new-tab) and uses minimal syntax to simulate the same behavior while avoiding more obvious patterns.
This command uses full path normalization and quotes to bypass detection by altering the command structure while maintaining the same behavior.
